examples.name: architecture_decision
examples.scenario: Developer discusses choosing a database for a new service.

prior_turns.speaker: user
prior_turns.text: We need a database for the new service

current_turn.speaker: user
current_turn.text: We decided to use PostgreSQL because we need ACID transactions and complex joins

examples.expected_output: [{"subject": "new_service", "predicate": "decision_made", "value": "use PostgreSQL for ACID transactions and complex joins", "confidence": 0.9}]

examples.name: bug_fix_record
examples.scenario: Developer describes a bug they just fixed.

prior_turns.speaker: user
prior_turns.text: I've been chasing a weird race condition in the payment queue all morning

current_turn.speaker: user
current_turn.text: Found it. The worker wasn't acking messages before the timeout, so RabbitMQ redelivered them. Fixed by increasing the prefetch timeout to 30s and adding idempotency keys.

examples.active_claims_summary: c_012: payment_queue.blocker = 'race condition causing duplicate processing' (confidence 0.85)
examples.expected_output: [{"subject": "payment_queue", "predicate": "bug_fixed", "value": "race condition from RabbitMQ redelivery — fixed by increasing prefetch timeout to 30s and adding idempotency keys", "confidence": 0.95}]

examples.name: convention_established
examples.scenario: Developer establishes a coding convention for the team.

prior_turns.speaker: assistant
prior_turns.text: How should I name the new API handler files?

current_turn.speaker: user
current_turn.text: All API handlers go in src/handlers/ and follow the pattern {resource}_handler.py. Use snake_case for everything.

examples.name: tool_preference
examples.scenario: Developer states a preference about their toolchain.

prior_turns.speaker: assistant
prior_turns.text: Should I set up the linter configuration?

current_turn.speaker: user
current_turn.text: Use ruff instead of flake8. It's way faster and we've standardized on it across all our Python repos.

examples.expected_output: [{"subject": "user", "predicate": "user_preference", "value": "use ruff over flake8 for Python linting — team standard", "confidence": 0.95}]

examples.name: project_status_update
examples.scenario: Developer gives a status update on the current project.

prior_turns.speaker: assistant
prior_turns.text: Where are we on the auth migration?

current_turn.speaker: user
current_turn.text: Auth migration is about 70% done. We've migrated the login and signup flows but still need to do password reset and OAuth providers. Target is end of next week.

examples.active_claims_summary: c_045: auth_migration.project_status = 'in progress, login flow migrated' (confidence 0.88)
examples.expected_output: [{"subject": "auth_migration", "predicate": "project_status", "value": "~70% complete — login and signup done, password reset and OAuth remaining, target end of next week", "confidence": 0.92}]

examples.name: file_purpose_documentation
examples.scenario: Developer explains what a specific file or module does.

prior_turns.speaker: assistant
prior_turns.text: I see there's a src/middleware/rate_limiter.py — what does it do?

current_turn.speaker: user
current_turn.text: rate_limiter.py implements a sliding-window rate limiter using Redis. It's middleware that wraps all public API endpoints and enforces per-tenant request quotas.

examples.expected_output: [{"subject": "src/middleware/rate_limiter.py", "predicate": "file_purpose", "value": "sliding-window rate limiter via Redis, enforces per-tenant request quotas on public API endpoints", "confidence": 0.95}]

examples.name: dependency_reason
examples.scenario: Developer explains why a specific dependency was added.

prior_turns.speaker: assistant
prior_turns.text: I noticed you added pydantic to the requirements. What's it for?

current_turn.speaker: user
current_turn.text: We need pydantic for request/response validation on the API layer. It gives us automatic OpenAPI schema generation too, which saves a ton of manual docs work.

examples.expected_output: [{"subject": "pydantic", "predicate": "dependency_reason", "value": "request/response validation on API layer + automatic OpenAPI schema generation", "confidence": 0.93}]
